column1: Sprint 1: Foundation: Authentication, Onboarding, Health Metrics
Sprint Goal: A new user can sign in (Google or Guest), complete onboarding, and see their calculated health metrics.

column5: As a user, I want to sign in with my Google account so that I can access the app quickly without creating a new password.
column6: Backend Auth service and User data model must exist before session issuance can be implemented.

column5: As a user, I want to use the app as a guest so that I can try it out before committing to creating an account.
column6: Depends on core User data model; upgrade path depends on Google Login (US-01.1.1) being implemented.

column5: As a user, I want to provide my basic profile and fitness information so that the app can personalize my workout recommendations.
column6: Requires User Authentication (EPIC-01) to associate profile data with an identity.

column5: As a user, I want to see my calculated BMI so that I understand a basic health reference point as I start using the app.
column6: Depends on Profile Data Collection (US-02.1.1) providing height and weight.

column5: As a user, I want to see my estimated Maximum Heart Rate so that I have a reference point for workout intensity.
column6: Depends on Profile Data Collection (US-02.1.1) providing age.

column5: As a user, I want to see my recommended training heart-rate zone so that I know how hard to push during workouts.
column6: Depends on Maximum Heart Rate Calculation (US-03.2.1).
